clinical_criteria: Culture drawn in outpatient setting or within 72 hours of admission as inpatient
No antecedent inpatient hospitalization in prior 1 year before MRSA infection
>=2 visits with primary care provider over minimum 3 year continuous period of enrollment
Continuous enrollment defined as at least 5 years allowing gaps up to 3 months
SSTI diagnosis within +/- 7 days of MRSA diagnosis (silver standard)
Culture positive for MRSA with keywords: methicillin OR oxacillin resistant
SSTI keywords: SKIN, WOUND, BOIL, ABSCESS, FOOT, HAND, LEG, BUTTOCK, BREAST, CARBUNCLE, FURUNCLE, FINGER, TOE, CELLULITIS, IMPETIGO, SUBCUTANEOUS, HAIR, HYDRADENITIS, NOSE, NASAL
algorithm_summary: This CA-MRSA phenotype algorithm identifies community-associated MRSA cases using two definitions: Gold standard requires culture-confirmed MRSA from outpatient/ED or within 72hrs of admission with SSTI site. Silver standard uses MRSA ICD-9 diagnosis codes with SSTI diagnosis within 7 days. Cases must be age 0-89 with no prior hospitalization in past year. Controls are patients age 0-89 with continuous primary care, no prior MRSA history, and no SSTI history. Algorithm emphasizes specificity over sensitivity and relies on EHR data including bacterial culture results, ICD-9 codes, and text mining for SSTI keywords.
